## Introduction

### Abe Sun, Ye Introduction Hai, Dhyan Se Padh Nahi Toh Baad Mein Royega

Bsdk, aaj-kal blockchain ki duniya mein har koi "Web3 developer" ban ke ghoom raha hai. Ek basic ERC-20 token bana liya, 10 lines ka contract copy-paste kar diya, aur bolo "Smart Contract Engineer". Bhai, teri aukaat tab tak nahi badhegi jab tak tu chain ke andar ka data real-world se nahi jodta. External data ke bina blockchain ek aisi bandh room hai jahan sab andar hi andar baat kar rahe hain, bahar ki duniya se zero connection. 

Agar tu advanced level ka dev ban'na chahta hai, agar tujhe "Hello World" wale chhote-mote contracts se pakwaar ho chuka hai, toh jagah se utth. Aaj hum banayenge ek **Decentralized Prediction Market**. Aur sun, ye koi simple betting ka khel nahi hai jo local bhai ke paas note register karke chal raha hota hai. Ye tamasha hoga ekdum trustless, transparent, aur code-driven. Bina kisi middleman ke, bina kisi bhai-ke-chaacha ke干涉, seedha smart contract pe. Ye introduction padh, dimaag khol, kyunki aage ka safar kandi (rocky) hai.

### Kya Banayega Tu? (What Readers Will Learn)

Sun, ye book teri amma ke ghar ki achar ki dukaan nahi hai jo sab kuch milega. Isme sirf wahi hai jo ek hardcore protocol engineer ko pata hona chahiye. Ye padh ke tu seekhega:

- **Oracle Integration ka Asli Jadu:** Chainlink oracles ko kaise configure karte hain, external data (jaise match ka score ya election result) ko on-chain kaise laate hain, aur price feeds ko kaise read karte hain bina kisi manipulation ke.
- **Mathematics of Shares:** Yes/No shares ko kaise mint karte hain, bonding curves ka formula kaise lagta hai, aur AMM (Automated Market Maker) logic ko contract ke andar kaise fit karte hain. Paise kaise lagte hain, kaise nikalte hain—sab math se.
- **State Machine Design:** Market kabhi "Open" hota hai, kabhi "Closed", kabhi "Resolving", aur kabhi "Resolved". Ek robust state machine kaise design karein ki ekdum gas-efficient rahe.
- **Security aur Reentrancy Guards:** Bhai, DeFi mein ek chook di, toh poora contract drain ho jayega. Reentrancy attacks, front-running, aur oracle manipulation se apne contract ko kaise bachayega, ye seekhega.
- **Testing Setup:** Hardhat environment pe kaise complex scenarios simulate karein, mainnet fork kaise karein, aur Chainlink mock contracts kaise deploy karke test karein.

### Roadmap: Safar Kaisa Hoga

Bhai, hum log seedha rasta nahi pakadte. Hum ek roadmap banate hain, aur usi pe chalte hain. Yahan tera poora syllabus hai:

1. **Bhai Ka ToolKit: Solidity Aur Hardhat Ka Bhandar** — Sabse pehle apne hathiyaar saaf kar. Solidity ke latest version, Hardhat setup, aur ekdum clean repository structure. Ye nahi karega toh baad mein dependencies ka error dekh ke tera dimaag phat jayega.
2. **Scene Palo: Market Ka Blueprint Taiyaar Kar** — Contract ka basic skeleton banayenge. Data structures, mappings, aur events define karenge. Market create karne ka function likhenge.
3. **Paise Lagao Boss: Betting Aur Share Minting Ka Khel** — Yahan asli logic aayega. User jab paise lagayega, tab Yes/No shares kaise mint honge? Liquidity kaise manage hogi? Math yahan tera dost bhi aur dushman bhi.
4. **Chainlink Ka Jadu: Bahar Ka Data Andar Kheench** — Yahan hum Chainlink oracle integrate karenge. Smart contract ko bahar ki duniya se jodenge. Request aur receive pattern, ya direct price feed consumption.
5. **Khel Khatam Paisa Bat: Market Resolve Aur Claim** — Market kaise resolve hoga? Oracle se jo result aayega, uske base pe losers ka paisa winners mein kaise distribute hoga? Claim function kaise likhe taaki gas cost kam lage.
6. **Bina Guard Ke Mat Jayio: Security Aur Reentrancy** — Ye chapter bachayega tujhe jail se. Reentrancy guards, access control, aur checks-effects-interactions pattern. Security pe koi compromise nahi.
7. **Pakka Wala Test: Mainnet Jaane Se Pehle Dimag Lagao** — Testnet pe deploy karne se pehle local testing. Edge cases, fuzzing, aur static analysis tools. Tab jaake mainnet pe jaane ka sochna.

### Kyu Padhe Ye? (Motivation)

Soch, agar tu ye skill seekh gaya, toh tu kisi Polymarket ya Omen ka clone bana sakta hai. Zyada soch, tu apna khud ka prediction protocol launch kar sakta hai. Web3 mein information asymmetry ka market bada hai. Log paise lagate hain future events pe. Agar tu ye contract bana sakta hai, toh tu DeFi ka core samajh gaya.

Aur haan, ye book advanced learners ke liye hai. Agar tujhe Solidity ke basics nahi aate, mapping kya hoti hai pata nahi, ya Hardhat kya cheez hai nahi pata, toh pehle wo seekh. Yahan hum direct meat pe aayenge. Koi haath nahi pakdayenge.

Toh chal, bhai ka toolkit khol, aur shuru karte hain is safar ko. Dhyan se padh, warna bug aayega toh poora paisa doob jayega.

# Module 1: Bhai Ka ToolKit: Solidity Aur Hardhat Ka Bhandar

Abe sun, raat ke 3 baj rahe hain. Tu screen ke aage baith kar "Web3 revolution" ka sapna dekh raha hai. Bhai tera ekdum se startup pe baith kar aag lagani hai, lekin pata hai tera kya haal hai? Tu aise react kar raha hai jaise local train mein bina ticket ghuse hai aur TC aage khada hai—bilkul ghabraya hua. 

Saale, pehle diya toh aag laga, baad mein chay banega. Tu seedha "Decentralized Prediction Market" banane ki bhashan deta hai. BC, market banane se pehle tere haath mein toh loha hona chahiye na? Sharma ji ka beta bhi pehle 'A,B,C' seekhta hai, tab jaakar IIT crack karta hai. Tu direct IIT mein baithne ki taiyaari kar raha hai bina padhe. 

Aaj hum naya karenge, lekin foundation pakka karenge. Is chapter mein hum apna **Bhai Ka ToolKit** taiyaar karenge—Solidity, Hardhat, aur ek basic token jiska use hum aage bet (paise) lagane ke kaam mein karenge. Agar ye chapter skip kiya toh tera poora project aise ud jayega jaise monsoon mein Mumbai ka pocha. Samjha? Nahi? Padh dubara BC.

Chal, aag lagate hain.

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

### 1. Hardhat: Teri Workshop
Dekh bhai, smart contract likhna ekdum aasan hai. Lekin usko compile karna, test karna, aur blockchain pe daalna—ye sab aise hai jaise tere ghar ka naali block ho gaya ho aur tu usko khud khol ke saaf kar raha ho. Bilkull ganda aur painful. Yahan aata hai **Hardhat**.

Hardhat teri workshop hai. Ye tera code compile karega, local blockchain ka node chalayega taaki tu bina paise diye apna contract test kar sake, aur testing ka environment dega. 

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Bina Hardhat (ya Foundry) ke direct mainnet pe contract deploy karna aise hai jaise bandh aankhon se Mahim creek cross kar raha ho. Ek kadam galat, aur paise doob. Hamesha local node pe pehle thok ke check kar.

**Setup Kar Bhai:**
Terminal khol aur ye commands daal. Pehle Node.js tera system mein hona chahiye, warna mat padhna iske aage.

```bash
mkdir prediction-market-gully
cd prediction-market-gully
npm init -y
npm install --save-dev hardhat
```

Ab seedha bootstrap karne ke liye ye maaro:
```bash
npx hardhat init
```
Isme poochega kya banana hai. Tu "Create a JavaScript project" select kar. Saale mat chalak banana ke TypeScript choose karke, warna type errors mein tera dimaag ghasal dega. JavaScript simple hai, tapori ke kaam ka hai. Enter, enter maarte ja, sab default rakho.

Ek baar setup ho gaya, toh tera folder aise dikhega:
- `contracts/` (Yahan aasli Solidity code rahega)
- `scripts/` (Ye script contract deploy karegi)
- `test/` (Yahan tu apna contract thokega/pitwayega)

### 2. Solidity: Tapori ki Bhasha
Solidity smart contract likhne ki language hai. Ye thodi C++ aur JavaScript jaisi dikhti hai, par iska dimaag alag hai. Isme ekdum se paise (wei/ether) handle hote hain. 

Dekh, prediction market ka asli khel kya hai? Log paise lagate hain (stake karte hain) "Haan" ya "Na" pe. Jaise "IPL final mein Mumbai Indians jeetegi?" Haan pe 1000 rupaye, Na pe 2000 rupaye. Jo jeetega, usko dusre ka paisa mil jayega (proportion mein).

Iske liye hume ek token chahiye. Asli ETH pe direct bet karna risky aur heavy hai. Hum ek apna ERC20 token banayenge—maano ye tapri ki chai ka token hai. Is token ko log khareedenge (ya testnet pe faucet se lenge) aur isse bet lagayenge.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Bahut saale direct `msg.value` (ETH) pe market banate hain. Bhai, ETH pe math lagana aur shares distribute karna gas cost mein tere dada ka property le lega. ERC20 token use kar, life simple rahegi.

### 3. ERC20 Token: Apna Sikka
Bhai, agar tu naya hai toh ERC20 standard aise samajh jaise Government ne note chhapne ka ek standard format diya hai. Har note ka size, watermark, paper same hoga. Waise hi blockchain pe har token ko ekdum standard banana padta hai taaki wallets (jaise MetaMask) isko pehchaan lein.

Hum OpenZeppelin ka use karenge. Ye aise hai jaise tujhe ready-made biryani masala mil gaya ho. Ghar pe sabzi kaatne ki zaroorat nahi. OpenZeppelin secure aur audit kiya hua code deta hai.

Install kar pehle:
```bash
npm install @openzeppelin/contracts
```

Ab apna token banate hain. `contracts/` folder mein ek file bana `GullyToken.sol` naam se.

```solidity
// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.20;

import "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/ERC20.sol";

contract GullyToken is ERC20 {
    constructor() ERC20("Gully Token", "GULLY") {
        // 1 Million tokens mint kar raha hu creator ko (matlab tujhko)
        _mint(msg.sender, 1000000 * 10 ** decimals());
    }
}
```

**Code ka Gyaan:**
- `pragma solidity ^0.8.20`: Ye batata hai kaunsi Solidity version use ho rahi hai. 0.8 se upar mein by default overflow/underflow check hota hai. Pehle ke zamaane mein saale hackers isse paise chura lete the.
- `is ERC20`: Ye inheritance hai. Bhai ne bola "Main ERC20 ka baap hoon", isse tera contract automatically saare standard token features (transfer, balance check) le leta hai.
- `constructor()`: Ye ek baar deploy hote waqt chalta hai. Humne token ka naam "Gully Token" aur symbol "GULLY" rakha.
- `_mint(msg.sender, ...)`: Deploy karte hi 10 lakh tokens tere (deployer ke) account mein daal diye. `decimals()` 18 hota hai ERC20 mein, isliye `10 ** 18` se multiply kiya. Samjha? Nahi? Chal, next.

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Ab workspace ready hai, loha garam hai. Chal thoda aag mein haath daalte hain. 

### 1. Local Node: Apna Private Adda
Mainnet pe deploy karne se pehle tujhe apne aas-paas ke logon pe test karna padta hai. Hardhat ek local blockchain node deta hai. Ye aise hai jaise apne ghar mein ek private adda bana liya, wahan tu jo chahe wo kar, bahar wale ko koi farak nahi padega.

Terminal mein ye command chala:
```bash
npx hardhat node
```

Dekh kya hota hai. Teri screen pe 20 accounts ban gaye, har ek mein 10,000 fake ETH hai. Ye tere gully ke test players hain. Is terminal ko band mat karna. Ek naya terminal khol aur aage badh.

### 2. Deployment Script: Contract Ko Chain Pe Utarna
Ab token contract likh liya, local node chal raha hai. Par ye token abhi tak blockchain pe nahi gaya. Usko "deploy" karna padega. `scripts/` folder mein jaa aur ek file bana `deploy.js`.

```javascript
const { ethers } = require("hardhat");

async function main() {
  // Contract factory load kar
  const GullyToken = await ethers.getContractFactory("GullyToken");
  
  console.log("Bhai, GullyToken deploy ho raha hai...");
  
  // Deploy kar
  const token = await GullyToken.deploy();
  await token.waitForDeployment();
  
  const address = await token.getAddress();
  console.log("GullyToken address pe deploy ho gaya:", address);
}

main()
  .then(() => process.exit(0))
  .catch((error) => {
    console.error("BC error aagaya:", error);
    process.exit(1);
  });
```

Ab isko local node pe chala:
```bash
npx hardhat run scripts/deploy.js --network localhost
```

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** Jab tu `--network localhost` lagata hai, toh Hardhat samajh jaata hai ki pehle se chal raha node (jo `npx hardhat node` se khula tha) pe contract daalna hai. Isse tu bina kisi testnet faucet ke poora system test kar sakta hai.

Console mein tu ek address dekhega. Ye tera token ka address hai. Isko yaad rakh, aage kaam aayega.

### 3. Thok Ke Test Kar (Sanity Check)
Bhai, code likh ke chhodne ka nahi. Pehle thok ke check kar ki bana toh sahi hai. Hum ek basic test likhte hain `test/` folder mein `gullyTokenTest.js` naam se.

```javascript
const { expect } = require("chai");
const { ethers } = require("hardhat");

describe("GullyToken", function () {
  it("Should mint 1 million tokens to deployer", async function () {
    const [owner] = await ethers.getSigners();
    const GullyToken = await ethers.getContractFactory("GullyToken");
    const token = await GullyToken.deploy();
    await token.waitForDeployment();

    const balance = await token.balanceOf(owner.address);
    // 1 million tokens with 18 decimals
    const expectedBalance = ethers.parseEther("1000000");
    
    expect(balance).to.equal(expectedBalance);
  });
});
```

Chala is test ko:
```bash
npx hardhat test
```

Agar green tick (✔) aaya, toh tu samajh le tera basic setup ekdum tight hai. Agar red aaya, toh upar ke steps dubara dekh, kahan galti ki tune.

### 4. Foundry Ka Chaska (Optional but Pro Level)
Bhai, Hardhat achha hai, par thoda slow hai. Agar tu real hacker ban na chahta hai, toh tujhe **Foundry** ka chaska lagna chahiye. Foundry Rust mein bana hai aur iski speed aisi hai jaise local train ka fast passenger. Hardhat mein test JavaScript/TypeScript mein likhte hain, lekin Foundry mein test Solidity mein likhte hain!

Matlab tu usi bhasha mein test likh raha hai jisme contract likha hai. Zero context switching. 

Foundry install karne ke liye:
```bash
curl -L https://foundry.paradigm.xyz | bash
foundryup
```

Foundry setup karne ka tareeka alag hai, par abhi ke liye Hardhat pe focus kar. Main tujhe Foundry ka idea isliye de raha hu kyunki aage ke chapters mein jab hum complex math aur Chainlink oracle test karenge, tab Foundry tujhe bachayega. 

Abhi ke liye, Hardhat tera aadmi hai. Usi ko sambhal.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Bhai, setup boring lagta hai. Main jaanta hu. Code likhne mein maza aata hai, npm install mein nahi. Par ye setup tere foundation ka cement hai. Agar cement kachra raha, toh building (contract) ekdum gir jayegi. Hacker ko 10 minute chahiye tere 10 din ke mehnat ko zero karne mein. Isliye aaj ka boring kaam kar, fer aage aake tu aaram se market ka design banayega.

### Aakhri Bhashan
Dekh, aaj tune kya kiya:
1. Hardhat ka workshop khola.
2. Solidity samjhi aur OpenZeppelin se ERC20 token banaya.
3. Local node chalaya aur deploy kiya.
4. Test likha aur apne code ko verify kiya.

Ye bahut basic hai, par sabse zaroori. Agle chapter mein hum is token ka use karke asli market ka blueprint banayenge. Market ka data structure design karenge. Wahan tujhe "Yes" aur "No" ke shares kaise manage karna hai, uska loha garam karenge. 

Agar ye chapter samajh nahi aaya, toh dobara padh. Code likh, errors dekh, aur local node chala. Tab tak apna tool kit sambhal ke rakh. Aage aur bhi bhari padhai hai.

# Module 2: Scene Palo: Market Ka Blueprint Taiyaar Kar

Abe sun, kal raat 3 baje ek banda code likh raha tha. Coffee ki 4 piping pi chuki hai. Dimag ka fuse ud raha hai. Usne socha "Chal bhai, prediction market bana leta hoon, aaj hi complete karunga." Usne sidha `Bhai Ka ToolKit` kholi, `Hardhat` ka server chalaya, aur direct `mapping` likhna shuru kar diya. Saale ne ek variable ka data type galat rakh diya. 200 line ka code likha, debug karne mein 5 ghante lag gaye. Pata chala `uint8` use kiya tha time ke liye, aur time 255 seconds ke baad wapas zero ho gaya. BC! Sherlocked tha saala apne hi code mein.

Ye hota hai jab tu bina blueprint ke ghar banane pe lag jaata hai. Pehle chapter mein tera `Bhai Ka ToolKit` set ho gaya, `Hardhat` chal raha hai, `Foundry` bhi ready hai test marne ke liye. Ab naya scene palne ka time hai. Aaj hum market ka naksha banayenge. Agar ye blueprint galti ban gaya, toh baad mein jo betting aur share minting ka khel khelna hai, wo sab kachra ho jaayega. Photocopy mat kar, dimaag lagaa.

Samjha? Nahi? Padh dubara BC.

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

Dekh bhai, ye aise samajh. Tu marriage hall book kar raha hai shaadi ke liye. Tujhe kya chahiye?
1. Hall ka address (Market ID).
2. Shaadi kab hai (Deadline).
3. Ladka hai ya ladki (Outcomes: YES/NO).
4. Kitne log aayenge aur kitne paise laayenge (Liquidity Pool aur Balances).

Agar tu ye chaar cheezein pehle se solid nahi karega, toh baarati aayenge aur pata chalega ki hall toh kisi aur ne book kar liya. Market ka data structure waise hi kaam karta hai. Ye teri jaan hai. Isko chhote se chhota galti mat dena.

### 1. Market Struct: Samaan Ka Thela

Prediction market ka core ek `struct` hai. Ye wo dabba hai jisme tere market ki saari jaankari rakhi jaayegi. Hum isko ekdum loha banayenge.

Saale, time hamesha `uint256` mein store karna. Kabhi `uint8` mat use karna time ke liye, warna market 4 minute mein expire ho jaayega. Unix timestamp use kar, Solidity ka `block.timestamp` ekdum mast kaam karta hai.

```solidity
struct Market {
    uint256 marketId;
    string question;
    uint256 deadline;
    bool isResolved;
    uint256 winningOutcome;
    uint256 totalYesShares;
    uint256 totalNoShares;
}
```

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** `question` ko `string` mein rakhna theek hai, lekin gas bachane ke liye agar fixed format ho sakta hai toh `bytes32` use kar. String storage mein mehenga padta hai bhai, har character ka hisaab hota hai.

Ab is struct ka ekdum perfect example dekh. Ye `Market` struct ekdum clean hai. `marketId` se pata chalega kaunsa market hai. `question` se pata chalega kis baat pe bet lag rahi hai. `deadline` se pata chalega kab tak bet lag sakti hai. `isResolved` batayega ki kya market ka result aa gaya hai. `winningOutcome` mein 0 ya 1 aayega (0 = NO, 1 = YES). `totalYesShares` aur `totalNoShares` se pata chalega kitne logon ne YES pe paisa lagaya hai aur kitne NO pe.

### 2. YES/NO Options: Mapping Ka Khel

Ab aate hain YES aur NO ke shares pe. Reader, sun, ye simple array nahi hai. Ye mapping hai. Mapping Solidity mein wo hai jo Mumbai ki local train ka route map hai—ekdum point-to-point, no bakwaas, direct pahunch.

```solidity
// Market ID => Outcome (0 for NO, 1 for YES) => User Address => Shares
mapping(uint256 => mapping(uint256 => mapping(address => uint256))) public userShares;
```

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** Dekh bhai, 3D mapping dikha tujhe? Market ID ke andar Outcome, ke andar User Address, ke andar Shares. Ye lagta hai complicated, par ye sabse gas-efficient tareeka hai kisi specific bande ke paas kitne shares hain, wo track karne ka. Array mein dhundhne mein O(n) time lagta hai, mapping mein O(1). Ekdam instant!

Bhai, `userShares` naam se mapping banaya hai. Pehla key `marketId` hai (kaunsa market). Doosra key `outcome` hai (YES ya NO). Teesra key user ka `address` hai (kaunsa banda). Aur value `uint256` hai (kitne shares). Jaise tu biryani order karta hai: "Bhai, Hyderabadi biryani (Market), Double masala (Outcome), mere naam ka parcel (Address), 2 kilo (Shares)". Ekdum clear, ekdum fast.

### 3. Liquidity Pool: Paise Ka Khazana

Ab aata hai sabse masaledaar hissa. Liquidity pool. Bhai, prediction market mein log tab tak bet nahi kar sakte jab tak usme paise (liquidity) na ho. Jaise autorickshaw wala tab tak gadi nahi chalega jab tak uske paas CNG na ho. Ye pool wo CNG hai.

Liquidity pool ka logic simple hai: total paise pool mein jaayenge, aur jab market resolve hoga, tab winners ko us pool se paisa millega baatne ka formula hisaab se.

```solidity
mapping(uint256 => uint256) public marketPool;
```

Ye simple si mapping hai. Market ID ke hisaab se total pool pata chal raha hai. Par iska design tujhe samajhna zaroori hai. Jab koi YES pe 100 rupee lagayega, toh ye 100 rupee pool mein add honge. Jab koi NO pe 50 rupee lagayega, toh ye 50 rupee bhi pool mein add honge. Total pool = 150 rupee.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Saale, pool mein paise daalte time reentrancy ka dhyan rakhna. Agar tu pool update karne se pehle shares transfer kar deta hai, toh hacker tera pool khali kar dega. Pehle state update, phir transfer. Ye golden rule hai. Iske baare mein Module 6 mein detail mein baat karenge, par abhi se dimaag mein daal le.

### 4. User Balances: Sharma Ji Ka Beta Track

Ab pata kaise chalega ki kiske paas kitna paisa hai? User balances track karne ka system banana padega. Ye waisa hai jaise Sharma ji apne bete ke kharche track karte hain—har ek paise ka hisaab.

```solidity
// User address => Market ID => Balance
mapping(address => mapping(uint256 => uint256)) public userBalances;
```

Bhai, ye mapping tujhe batayega ki kisi bande ne kisi market mein kitna paisa lagaya hai. Aur jab market resolve hoga, toh isme se winning amount add hoga. Simple.

Par ekdum dhyan se sun. Ye `userBalances` sirf us bande ka invested paisa track karta hai. Isme profit ya loss ka hisaab nahi hota. Profit/loss tab calculate hota hai jab market resolve hota hai. Tab `userShares` aur `winningOutcome` milake formula lagta hai. Samjha? Nahi? Padh dubara BC, itna easy hai ki tera pet ka kutta bhi samajh jaaye.

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Ab theory band. Ab practical scene palte hain. Tu samajh gaya struct aur mapping kya hota hai. Ab inko ekdum code mein utaar te hain. Dekh, agar tu ye chapter skip karta hai, toh baad mein betting aur share minting ka code likhte time tu galti karega aur pata bhi nahi chalega. ### Code Ka Gyaan: Pura Blueprint

Apne `PredictionMarket.sol` file khol. Aur ye poora code daal.

```solidity
// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.19;

contract PredictionMarket {
    
    // 1. Market Struct: Samaan ka thela
    struct Market {
        uint256 marketId;
        string question;
        uint256 deadline;
        bool isResolved;
        uint256 winningOutcome;
        uint256 totalYesShares;
        uint256 totalNoShares;
    }
    
    // 2. Mappings: YES/NO options, Pool, Balances
    mapping(uint256 => Market) public markets;
    mapping(uint256 => mapping(uint256 => mapping(address => uint256))) public userShares;
    mapping(uint256 => uint256) public marketPool;
    mapping(address => mapping(uint256 => uint256)) public userBalances;
    
    // Owner address for admin controls
    address public owner;
    
    // Counter for market IDs
    uint256 public nextMarketId;
    
    // 3. Constructor: Setup kar bhai
    constructor() {
        owner = msg.sender;
        nextMarketId = 0;
    }
    
    // 4. Create Market: Naya market khool
    function createMarket(string memory _question, uint256 _durationInMinutes) external {
        require(_durationInMinutes > 0, "BC, duration zero kaise ho sakta hai?");
        require(bytes(_question).length > 0, "Question toh likh saale");
        
        uint256 marketId = nextMarketId;
        markets[marketId] = Market({
            marketId: marketId,
            question: _question,
            deadline: block.timestamp + (_durationInMinutes * 1 minutes),
            isResolved: false,
            winningOutcome: 0,
            totalYesShares: 0,
            totalNoShares: 0
        });
        
        nextMarketId++;
    }
    
    // 5. Buy Shares: Paise lagao boss
    function buyShares(uint256 _marketId, uint256 _outcome) external payable {
        Market storage market = markets[_marketId];
        
        require(_marketId < nextMarketId, "Market exist nahi karta");
        require(block.timestamp < market.deadline, "Deadline guzar gayi hai");
        require(_outcome == 0 || _outcome == 1, "Sirf YES (1) ya NO (0)");
        require(msg.value > 0, "Paise toh de");
        
        // Update pool
        marketPool[_marketId] += msg.value;
        
        // Update user balance
        userBalances[msg.sender][_marketId] += msg.value;
        
        // Mint shares (1:1 ratio for now, pricing logic baad mein)
        if (_outcome == 1) {
            market.totalYesShares += msg.value;
            userShares[_marketId][1][msg.sender] += msg.value;
        } else {
            market.totalNoShares += msg.value;
            userShares[_marketId][0][msg.sender] += msg.value;
        }
    }
    
    // 6. Get Market Info: Pata lagao kya scene hai
    function getMarketInfo(uint256 _marketId) external view returns (
        string memory question,
        uint256 deadline,
        bool isResolved,
        uint256 winningOutcome,
        uint256 totalPool,
        uint256 yesShares,
        uint256 noShares
    ) {
        Market storage market = markets[_marketId];
        return (
            market.question,
            market.deadline,
            market.isResolved,
            market.winningOutcome,
            marketPool[_marketId],
            market.totalYesShares,
            market.totalNoShares
        );
    }
}
```

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** Dekh bhai, `buyShares` function mein `msg.value` use kiya hai. Ye Solidity ka built-in variable hai jo batata hai ki user ne kitna Ether bheja hai. Aur `payable` keyword lagana mat bhoolna, warna Ether accept nahi hoga. Aur `require` statements lagani mat bhoolna, warna koi 0 Ether bhej dega aur share le jaayega. BC, zero rupee mein share? Aisa chance mat de.

### Breakdown: Ek-Ek Line Ka Postmortem

Ab postmortem karte hain is code ka. Ek-ek line samajh, warna baad mein rona mat.

**1. Struct aur Mappings:**
Pehle humne `Market` struct banaya. Phir humne 4 mappings banaye: `markets` (market ID se struct tak), `userShares` (3D mapping), `marketPool` (market ID se total pool tak), aur `userBalances` (user address aur market ID se balance tak). Ye tera data layer hai. Jaise bank ka locker, jisme paise aur documents rakhe hain.

**2. Constructor:**
`constructor()` mein humne `owner` ko `msg.sender` set kiya. Jo contract deploy karega, wo owner hoga. Aur `nextMarketId` ko 0 set kiya. Simple.

**3. Create Market:**
`createMarket` function mein do cheezein chahiye: `_question` (string) aur `_durationInMinutes` (uint256). Pehle `require` check lagaya ki duration zero na ho. Doosra `require` check ki question empty na ho. Phir `block.timestamp` mein duration add karke deadline set ki. `block.timestamp` current block ka Unix timestamp hai. Isko `1 minutes` se multiply kiya (Solidity mein `1 minutes` ek time unit hai jo 60 seconds ke barabar hota hai). Phir `nextMarketId` increment kiya.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** `block.timestamp` kabhi exact second nahi hota. Ye current block ka timestamp hai. Ethereum mein ek block ~12 second mein banta hai. Toh agar tu deadline 1 minute rakhta hai, toh actually market 60-72 seconds tak active rahega. Critical time-based logic mein ye 12-second buffer d

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Saale, `Market storage market = markets[_marketId]` likhna. `memory` mat likhna. `storage` use karne se gas bachta hai kyunki struct direct storage mein update hota hai. `memory` use kiya toh gas double lagega aur state update nahi hogi. Ye rookie mistake hai, Sharma ji ke bete se mat pooch.

**4. Buy Shares:**
`buyShares` function mein pehle `require` checks lagaye. Market exist karta hai ya nahi? Deadline guzar toh nahi gayi? Outcome 0 ya 1 hai? Paise diye ya nahi? Phir `marketPool` update kiya. `userBalances` update kiya. Phir `if-else` lagake YES shares mint kiye ya NO shares mint kiye.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Bhai, abhi humne 1:1 ratio pe shares mint kiye (1 Ether = 1 Share). Ye simple hai. Par asli prediction market mein pricing formula hota hai (jaise AMM - Automated Market Maker). Uska formula thoda complex hota hai. Par abhi ke liye 1:1 ratio mast hai, kyunki pehle blueprint solid karna hai. Fancy pricing baad mein.

**5. Get Market Info:**
`getMarketInfo` ek `view` function hai. Ye gas nahi leta. Tu isse frontend pe market ka data dikha sakta hai. Question, deadline, pool, shares—sab kuch ek hi function se pata chal jaayega. Ekdum clean.

### Gas Optimization: Chai ki Tapri Wali Economics

Bhai, gas bachana hai, nahi toh users ko har bet pe 5-10 dollar ka gas fee dena padega. Koi nahi aayega ter

1. **Storage slots:** `Market` struct mein fields ka order important hai. Solidity mein 256-bit (32 bytes) slots pack hoti hain. `uint256` (32 bytes) ek slot leta hai. `bool` (1 byte) aur `uint256` ko alag-alag slots mein daalna padega, kyunki `uint256` ek poora slot leta hai. Par `bool` (1 byte) aur `uint8` (1 byte) ko ek slot mein pack kar sakte hain. Gas bachao bhai, warna users bhag jaayenge.

2. **`storage` vs `memory`:** Function ke andar struct access karte time `storage` use kar. `Market storage market = markets[_marketId]` likhna. `memory` mat likhna. `storage` use karne se gas bachta hai kyunki struct direct storage mein update hota hai. `memory` use kiya toh gas double lagega aur state update nahi hogi. Ye rookie mistake hai, Sharma ji ke bete se mat pooch.

3. **Custom Errors:** `require` statements mein string mat use kar. Custom errors use kar. Gas bachta hai. Par abhi ke liye `require` string ke saath chal, kyunki debugging easy hota hai. Baad mein optimize kar denge.

4. **`view` functions:** `getMarketInfo` jaise `view` functions gas nahi lete. Frontend pe inka free mein use kar. Par state change karne wale functions (jaise `buyShares`) gas lete hain. Toh inko optimize rakh.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Mapping ka key data type hamesha `uint256` ya `address` rakhna. String ko key mat bana, warna keccak256 hash karna padega aur gas l

### Testing Ka Scene: Foundry Se Thok Ke Check

Bhai, code likhne ke baad test nahi likhega toh kya faida? Foundry se test marte hain. Apne `test/PredictionMarket.t.sol` file khol aur ye code daal.

```solidity
        pm.createMarket("Ethereum merge hoga?", 1 minutes);

        vm.warp(block.timestamp + 2 minutes);

        vm.expectRevert("Deadline guzar gayi hai");
        vm.prank(address(1));
        pm.buyShares{value: 1 ether}(0, 1);
    }
    `);
    }
    
    function testInvalidOutcome() public {
        pm.createMarket("Test market", 60);
```

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Saale, Foundry mein time travel ke liye `vm.warp()` use karna. `block.timestamp` ko direct change nahi kar sakte. `vm.warp()` se time fast-forward kar. Aur `vm.prank()` se fake address se transaction bhej. Ye cheats hai Foundry ke, inka use karna seekh.

### Test Breakdown: Kya Ho Raha Hai

Dekh bhai, ye tests tujhe dikhate hain ki tera blueprint ka

1. **testCreateMarket:** Market ban raha hai ya nahi? Question sahi se store ho raha hai ya nahi? IPL wala example liya, kyunki sabko IPL se pyaar hai.
2. **testBuyShares:** User YES shares kharid raha hai ya nahi? Pool update ho raha hai ya nahi? `vm.deal` se fake Ether diya address 1 ko. `vm.prank` se address 1 se transaction bheji. 0.5 Ether diya, YES (1) outcome pe.
3. **testDeadlineExpiry:** Deadline ke baad koi bet nahi kar sakta. `vm.warp` se 2 minute fast-forward kiya. Phir `vm.expectRevert` se check kiya ki function revert hoga ya nahi. Agar revert hua, toh test pass.
4. **testInvalidOutcome:** Agar koi 0 ya 1 ke alawa outcome pe bet kare, toh revert ho jaana chahiye. 2 outcome pe bet kiye, revert expect kiya. Simple.

### Common Pitfalls: Galtiyan Jo Sab Karte Hain

Ab sun, ye wo galtiyan hain jo har rookie karta hai. Inko avoid kar, warna gaali khayega.

1. **Zero Address Check:** `createMarket` mein owner check mat bhool. Agar tu owner check nahi karega, toh koi bhi market bana sakta hai. Bhai, tera market hai, tu decide karega kaunsa market banega. `require(msg.sender == owner, "Tu owner nahi hai");` ye line add kar dena `createMarket` mein. Wait, actually `createMarket` ko public rakh sakte hain, agar tu chahta hai ki koi bhi market bana sake. Par agar tu admin control chahta hai, toh owner check add kar.

2. **Reentrancy:** `buyShares` mein pehle state update karo (pool, balances, shares), phir koi external call karo. Abhi humare code mein koi external call nahi hai, par baad mein Chainlink oracle aayega. Tab dhyan rakhna. Pehle state, phir call. Golden rule.

3. **Integer Overflow/Underflow:** Solidity 0.8.x mein overflow/underflow automatically check hota hai. Toh chinta mat kar. Par agar tu 0.7.x use kar raha hai, toh `SafeMath` library use kar. Bhai, 0.8.x use kar, 0.7.x mein kya rakha hai.

4. **Timestamp Dependence:** `block.timestamp` pe 100% mat bhar. Miner timestamp ko thoda manipulate kar sakta hai (15 seconds tak). Toh critical decisions `block.timestamp` ke alone pe mat lena. Par deadline ke liye ye theek hai, 15 seconds ka issue nahi hai.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Bhai, `payable` keyword mat bhoolna `buyShares` function mein. Agar `payable` nahi lagaayega, toh contract Ether accept nahi karega. Aur `msg.value` zero aayega. Log paise bhejenge, contract reject karega, log galiyan denge. Mat kar aisi galti.

# Module 3: Paise Lagao Boss: Betting Aur Share Minting Ka Khel

Abe sun, raat ke 3 baj rahe hain. Tu coffee peeta hai, code run karta hai, aur error aate hi rota hai. "Bhai error kyun aa raha hai?" Saale dimag lagaa, gas limit ki jagah tu apni life ka limit khaa raha hai. Pichle chapter mein tera market ka blueprint (`Struct aur Mappings:`, `Constructor:`, `Create Market:`) toh set ho gaya. Ab waqt hai asli khel ka. Ab paise aane hain. 

Dekh bhai, prediction market ka asli maza tab hai jab log apne paise lagayein. Ek side bol raha hai "Modi jeetega", doosra side bol rahi hai "Nahi BC, Rahul aayega". Tu asli bookie nahi hai, tu ek decentralized harami hai. Tujhe aisa system banana hai ki log paise daalein, aur unke hisaab se shares milen. Agar tere contract mein bug nikla, toh hacker tera pura contract khali karke Baga beach pe cocktail peene chala jayega, aur tu footpath pe ro raha hoga.

Isliye, aaj ka chapter "Paise Lagao Boss: Betting Aur Share Minting Ka Khel" — isko dhyaan se padh. Ye teri contract ki jaan hai, warna `Integer Overflow/Underflow` aur `Reentrancy` jaise saale aake tera poora system barbaad kar denge.

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

Bhai, betting ka system samajh pehle. Tere paas do outcomes hain: `Yes` (0) aur `No` (1). Jab koi paise lagata hai, toh use ek "Share" milta hai. Ye share wahi hai jo local train ka ticket hota hai — agar tu destination pohch gaya (matlab outcome sahi nikla), toh ticket ka pura paisa mil jayega. Agar galat raha, toh ticket kachre mein.

Ab pricing ka dhanda. IIT wale ch**te AMM (Automated Market Maker) ka formula lagate hain, par hum simple rkhenge. Kyun? Kyunki over-engineering karna chutiyaapan hai. Hum **Fixed-Odds Pricing** use karenge. Matlab, ek share ka price fix hai: `0.01 ETH`. Simple, clean, aur hacker ko bhi dimag khaana padega.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Fixed-odds mein 1 share = fixed price (jaise 0.01 ETH). Jab market resolve hoga, jitne shares winning side ke hain, total pool mein se unka hisaab baat jayega. Ye `x*y=k` wale AMM se zyada safe aur predictable hai beginner ke liye.

### 1. BuyShares Function: Paise Kaise Aayenge?

Ye function tera hero hai. Iska kaam simple hai:
1. Dekh user kis outcome pe bet kar raha hai (`Yes` ya `No`).
2. Dekh kitne paise de raha hai.
3. Uske hisaab se shares calculate kar (e.g., 0.01 ETH = 1 share).
4. Shares uske account mein daal de.
5. Total pool update kar de.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Bhai, function mein `payable` keyword bhool jaata hai. Agar `payable` nahi likhega, toh contract paise khaayega hi nahi. Fir log `msg.value` bhejenge aur error aayega "revert". Fir tu royyega "BC paise kyun nahi aaye?" Saale, paise lene ke liye bolna padta hai, izzat se nahi milti.

Dekh iska code, aur dimaag lagaa:

```solidity
// Pichle chapter se aaya hua structure yaad rakho
// struct Market { ... }
// mapping(uint256 => Market) public markets;

uint256 public constant SHARE_PRICE = 0.01 ether;

function buyShares(uint256 _marketId, uint8 _outcome) external payable {
    Market storage market = markets[_marketId];
    
    // 1. Zero Address Check aur Market existence (Pichle chapter se aata hai)
    require(_marketId < nextMarketId, "Market nahi mila BC");
    
    // 2. Outcome valid hai ya nahi?
    require(_outcome == 0 || _outcome == 1, "Galat outcome saale");
    
    // 3. Market open hai ya band?
    require(block.timestamp < market.deadline, "Deadline khatam ho gayi");
    
    // 4. Paise toh la saale!
    require(msg.value > 0, "Bina paise ka mat khel");
    
    // 5. Shares calculate karo (Fixed Price)
    uint256 sharesToMint = msg.value / SHARE_PRICE;
    require(sharesToMint > 0, "Itna paisa toh ek share ka bhi nahi");
    
    // 6. Shares user ko de do
    userShares[_marketId][_outcome][msg.sender] += sharesToMint;
    
    // 7. Market pool update karo
    if (_outcome == 0) {
        market.totalYesShares += sharesToMint;
        market.totalYesPool += msg.value;
    } else {
        market.totalNoShares += sharesToMint;
        market.totalNoPool += msg.value;
    }
    
    // 8. Event emit karo (Ispe baad mein baat karenge)
    emit SharesBought(_marketId, msg.sender, _outcome, sharesToMint, msg.value);
}
```

Dekh, upar ka code dekh. `Market storage market = markets[_marketId];` — ye `storage vs memory:` ka khel hai. Hum `storage` use kar rahe hain kyunki market ka data permanently update karna hai. Agar `memory` kar deta, toh changes temporary reh jaate, aur blockchain pe kuch save nahi hota. Fir tu `testBuyShares:` run karega aur dekhega balance 0 hai. Fir hasi sab pe.

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** `msg.value` hamesha `wei` mein aata hai. `0.01 ether` Solidity mein automatically `wei` mein convert ho jata hai (10000000000000000 wei). Isliye division `(msg.value / SHARE_PRICE)` se exact number of shares mil jate hain. Decimal aur fraction ka chakkar nahi.

### 2. Paise Ka Hisaab: Pool Aur Shares Ka Jugaad

Bhai, ek cheez samajh. Tera contract dikhawa nahi karta. Jo balance contract pe hai, wahi uski aukaat hai. Agar tu `totalYesPool` aur `totalNoPool` ka hisaab nahi rakhaga, toh market resolve hone ke baad paise kaise baatoge? Sharma ji ke bete ki tarah marks la lekin placement na lage, toh kya fayda?

Jab user paise daalega, hum do cheezein update karte hain:
1. `userShares`: Kis user ke paas kitne shares hain.
2. `totalYesPool` / `totalNoPool`: Total kitna paisa dono sides pe jama hua hai.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Tera contract balance (`address(this).balance`) tere `totalYesPool + totalNoPool` ke barabar hona chahiye. Agar nahi hai, toh koi saala bug hai. Pakad ke usko thok. `testBuyShares:` mein ye check karlena ki contract ka balance badha ya nahi.

Agar koi 1 ETH daalta hai `Yes` pe, toh `totalYesPool` 1 ETH badh jayega. Aur usko 100 shares mil jayenge (kyunki 1 ETH / 0.01 ETH = 100). Simple math. Agar ye math nahi aata, toh coding chhod ke chai bech.

### 3. Events: Kyun Zaroori Hai? Transparency BC!

Bhai, chain pe sab kuch transparent hona chahiye. Tere contract mein paise daalte hi ek event emit hona chahiye. Jaise hi koi bet kare, ek tamasha (event) machao. Kyun? Taaki frontend (React app) pe log dekh sakein ki "Abe Ramesh ne 5 ETH lagaye Yes pe!" Ye trust banata hai.

Events gas cost bachate hain kyunki unka data storage mein nahi, logs mein save hota hai. Frontend wale bahar se `eth_getLogs` se ye data kheench lenge.

```solidity
event SharesBought(
    uint256 indexed marketId, 
    address indexed buyer, 
    uint8 outcome, 
    uint256 sharesAmount, 
    uint256 amountPaid
);
```

Dekh `indexed` keyword. Iska fayda hai ki jab koi frontend pe filter lagayega "Market 1 pe kaun kaun ne bet kiya?", toh ye directly search ho jayega. Jaise Google mein search karte hain na? Waise.

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Chalo, ab tapri pe baith ke practical scene samajhte hain. Tu contract deploy kar chuka hai. Ab tere dost Mangesh aur Suresh khelne aate hain.

**Scene 1: Mangesh 0.05 ETH daalta hai `Yes` pe.**
- `msg.value` = 0.05 ETH
- `SHARE_PRICE` = 0.01 ETH
- `sharesToMint` = 0.05 / 0.01 = 5 shares.
- `userShares[1][0][Mangesh]` = 5.
- `totalYesShares` = 5.
- `totalYesPool` = 0.05 ETH.
- `emit SharesBought(...)`

Bhai, kya hua? Samajh nahi aaya? Nahi? Padh dubara, dimag lagaa. Ye math 5th standard ka hai. Agar 0.05 ETH pe 5 shares mil rahe hain, toh tera math sahi hai. Agar 0.05 ETH pe 50 shares mil rahe hain, toh tu ch**tiya hai, apna code check kar.

**Scene 2: Suresh 0.1 ETH daalta hai `No` pe.**
- `msg.value` = 0.1 ETH
- `SHARE_PRICE` = 0.01 ETH
- `sharesToMint` = 0.1 / 0.01 = 10 shares.
- `userShares[1][1][Suresh]` = 10.
- `totalNoShares` = 10.
- `totalNoPool` = 0.1 ETH.
- `emit SharesBought(...)`

Ab total contract balance = 0.05 (Mangesh) + 0.1 (Suresh) = 0.15 ETH.
Aur tera `totalYesPool (0.05) + totalNoPool (0.1) = 0.15 ETH`.
Bingo! Hisaab barabar. Agar ye hisaab barabar nahi hai, toh hacker ne pehle hi maar liya.

### Edge Cases: Jinko Bhul Gaya Toh G**d Mar Di

Bhai, smart contract mein ek chook di, toh band baj jayegi. Yahan koi "sorry" nahi chalta. Dekh in cheezon ka dhyaan rakh:

1. **Deadline Expiry:** Agar market ka time khatam ho gaya, toh koi bet nahi kar sakta. `require(block.timestamp < market.deadline)` ye line bahut zaroori hai. Pichle chapter mein tune `testDeadlineExpiry:` likha tha, wahi test yahan bhi kaam aayega. Agar deadline ke baad paise daalne de raha hai, toh tu apne aap ko bookie nahi, ch**tiya samajh raha hai.

2. **Invalid Outcome:** Koi saala `2` ya `69` outcome pe bet karne ki koshish kare. `require(_outcome == 0 || _outcome == 1)`. Ye line nahi hai toh contract break ho jayega. `testInvalidOutcome:` yaad hai? Wahi logic.

3. **Zero Value Transaction:** Koi bina paise `buyShares` call kare. `require(msg.value > 0)`. Bina paise ka ticket kahan milta hai? Footpath pe bhi nahi.

4. **Dust Paise:** Agar koi 0.009 ETH bhejta hai, toh `sharesToMint` zero ban jayega (kyunki integer division). Isliye `require(sharesToMint > 0)` lagana padta hai. Bachat karne wale ko gaali do.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Bhai, integer division bhool jaate hain. Solidity mein 5 / 2 = 2 hota hai, 2.5 nahi. Agar `msg.value` thoda kam hai, toh share zero ban jayega. User ka paisa contract mein phas jayega. Isliye `require(sharesToMint > 0)` lagana padta hai. Bachat karne wale ko gaali do.

### Reentrancy Ka Dar

Bhai, pichle chapter mein tune `Reentrancy:` padha tha. Ab yahan uska asli darr hai. Jab paise aate hain, toh hacker soch sakta hai ki "Abe `buyShares` mein koi loophole nikal ke pura contract khali kar du." Lekin dekh, hamare `buyShares` function mein hum paise *bahar* nahi bhej rahe. Hum sirf andar le rahe hain. Isliye reentrancy ka risk zyada nahi hai. Lekin fir bhi, `Checks-Effects-Interactions` pattern follow kar rahe hain. Pehle checks (`require`), fir effects (shares update), fir interactions (events). 

Agar tu bina soche-samjhe paise wapas karta (jo ki next chapter `Market Resolve` mein hoga), toh wahan reentrancy ka risk hota. Isliye, `buyShares` function ko safe rakh. `Foundry` ya `Hardhat` se `testBuyShares:` likh, aur ek hacker ki tarah attack karke dekh ki kya ye function do baar chal sakta hai? Agar haan, toh tera contract g**du hai.

### Tera Code, Tera Zimma

Bhai, ye `buyShares` function tera asteet (existence) hai. Ek line galat likhi, toh lakhon rupaye drain. Isliye, code likhne se pehle, chai ki tapri pe baith aur flow chart bana. Pehle checks, fir math, fir state update. Samjha? Nahi samjha? Padh dubara BC. Ye 3rd chapter hai, abhi 4 aur bachhe hain. Agar yahan rona hai toh better hai tu sharma ji ke bete ki tarah job ki talash kar le. Asli hacker banne ke liye dimag aur chai, dono chahiye. Chai pi, code likh, aur gaali khaa. Aage!

# Module 4: Chainlink Ka Jadu: Bahar Ka Data Andar Kheench

Abe sun, tera bhai local train mein 6:17 ke Churchgate fast mein travel kar raha hai. Bheed itni tight hai ki saamne wale bande ki wallet ki khushboo aa rahi hai. Suddenly train thodi hilti hai, tu hilta hai, aur tu apne dimaag mein sochta hai: "Aaj India match jeetega kya?" Tu apne phone pe Dream11 ya kisi betting app pe check karta hai. Lekin ruk... ye prediction market jo tu bana raha hai, isko kaise pata chalega ki India jeeta ya nahi? Smart contract toh andha hai, BC. Wo blockchain ke andar band hai. Usne match dekha nahi, TV pe nahi hai, Twitter pe scroll nahi karta.

Saale, ye sabse crucial chapter hai. Agar ye skip kiya toh tera pura prediction market ek dumb, andha, bekaar piece of code ban ke reh jaayega jiska koi matlab nahi hai. Tu market bana dega, log paise lagayenge, lekin jab result aana hoga... contract bolega "Bhai main toh andar band hoon, mujhe kya pata India jeeta ya Australia?" 

Isliye aaj hum bahar ki duniya ka data blockchain ke andar kheenchenge. Chainlink ka jadu hai ye. Tyaar ho ja, chai pi le, aur dhyan se padh. Ye chapter teri prediction market ki aatma hai.

---

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

Dekh bhai, ye aise samajh... jaise tu ek room mein band hai. Kamra bahut accha hai, AC hai, Netflix hai, khana hai. Lekin tujhe bahar ka mausam pata nahi. Tu khidki se bahar dekh nahi sakta. Tu kisi ko phone karega aur poochega "Bhai bahar baarish ho rahi hai kya?" Jo banda phone uthake tujhe mausam bataayega, wo tera **Oracle** hai.

Blockchain ek closed room hai. Smart contracts khidki nahi dekh sakte. Wo bahar ki duniya—stock price, cricket match result, election result—kuch nahi dekh sakte. Inhe bahar ka data chahiye hota hai decisions lene ke liye. Yahan aata hai **Oracle**.

### Oracle Ka Pehla Funda: Centralized Oracle Mat Bana, BC
Pehla sawal: "Bhai, main ek simple API bana dunga, wo internet se data uthayega aur smart contract ko de dega. Itni kya bakwaas hai?"

Saale, agar tera contract ek hi API pe depend karta hai, toh wo decentralized nahi raha. Agar tera server hack ho gaya, ya tune galti se galat data bhej diya, ya tu chala gaya Fiji island vacation pe aur band kar diya API, toh lakho rupaye logon ke doob jayenge. Isko **Single Point of Failure** bolte hain. 

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Centralized oracle pe bharosa karna. "Apna API bana lunga" wali soch chhod de. Ek hack ho gaya aur tera pura reputation gutter mein.

Isliye hum use karte hain **Chainlink**. Chainlink ek decentralized network hai. Bahut saare nodes bahar se data uthate hain, consensus karte hain, aur phir ek verified data blockchain pe daalte hain. Koi ek banda isko manipulate nahi kar sakta. 

### Chainlink Data Feeds: Direct Sachai Ka Pipe
Chainlink ka sabse famous feature hai **Data Feeds**. Ye pre-built pipelines hain jo continuously real-world data (jaise BTC/USD price, ETH/USD price, etc.) update karte hain on-chain. 

Hum apne prediction market ke liye ishi ka use karenge. Maan le tu ek market bana raha hai: "Kya Bitcoin $100,000 cross karega 31st December tak?" 
Market resolve karne ke liye tujhe exact BTC ka price chahiye us time pe. Chainlink Data Feed se hum direct `latestRoundData()` nikal lenge.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Chainlink Data Feeds sirf price ke liye nahi hote. Bahut saare feeds available hain—sports, weather, random numbers. Hum price feed use karke concept samjhenge kyunki ye sabse stable aur easy hai.

### AggregatorV3Interface: Chainlink Ka Remote Control
Chainlink ke data feeds ek alag contract pe chalte hain. Hum apne contract se us contract ko "call" karte hain. Iske liye Chainlink ne ek interface diya hai: `AggregatorV3Interface`.

Interface kya hota hai? Soch, tu autorickshaw mein baitha hai. Tujhe driver ko exact engine ka mechanism nahi pata, bas meter pe bhaav dekhna hai aur paise dena hai. Interface wahi hai—wo batata hai "Bhai, ye function call karoga toh ye data milega, andar kaise kaam karta hai tu mat soch."

Isko use karne ke liye hume Chainlink ke contracts install karne padenge. Yaad hai **Bhai Ka ToolKit** mein humne Hardhat setup kiya tha? Wahan hum Foundry/Hardhat ke through Chainlink install karenge.

```bash
npm install @chainlink/contracts
```

Ya agar tu Foundry (wo speed wala tool) use kar raha hai:
```bash
forge install smartcontractkit/chainlink-brownie-contracts
```

Ab apne contract ke top pe isko import kar:
```solidity
import "@chainlink/contracts/src/v0.8/shared/interfaces/AggregatorV3Interface.sol";
```

Bas, ab tera contract Chainlink ke data feed se baat karne ke liye ready hai. Ek baat dhyan rakh, Data Feed ka address har network pe alag hota hai. Mumbai testnet pe alag, Mainnet pe alag. Tujhe constructor mein wo address pass karna padega.

### The `latestRoundData()` Function: Asli Maal
Jab tu Chainlink se data maangta hai, toh tu `latestRoundData()` function call karta hai. Ye function tujhe 5 cheezein return karta hai. Dekh carefully, kyunki 80% log yahan galti karte hain.

```solidity
(
    uint80 roundId,
    int256 answer,
    uint256 startedAt,
    uint256 updatedAt,
    uint80 answeredInRound
) = priceFeed.latestRoundData();
```

1. **roundId:** Konsa round chal raha hai. (Humko iski zarurat nahi).
2. **answer:** Asli price! (Ye chahiye humko).
3. **startedAt:** Round kab shuru hua.
4. **updatedAt:** Round kab update hua. **BC, ye sabse important hai.**
5. **answeredInRound:** Internal use.

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** `answer` ek `int256` mein aata hai, `uint256` mein nahi. Kyunki price negative nahi hota, but Chainlink standard format `int256` use karta hai. Isko `uint256` mein convert karte time sambhal ke typecast kar, warna contract revert marega.

### Oracle Staleness Check: Fake Data Se Bachao
Ab aate hain asli street smartness pe. Saale, tu soch raha hai "Bhai, Chainlink toh decentralized hai, data hamesha sahi aayega, main bas `answer` nikal lunga aur market resolve kar dunga."

Ruk bhai. Tu local train mein hai, train achanak track pe kisi gaay ke wajah se ruk gayi. 2 ghante platform pe khada raha. Kya tu bolega train abhi bhi chal rahi hai? Nahi. Time ruk gaya.

Chainlink ke data feeds kabhi-kabhi update nahi hote. Network congestion, oracle node failure, ya kisi aur chakkar wo data purana ho sakta hai. Isko **Stale Data** bolte hain. Agar tu bina check kiye `answer` use karke market resolve kar gaya, aur wo data 3 din purana hai... toh kisi ne hack karke purana price deke paise nikal liye.

Isliye hume `updatedAt` timestamp check karna padta hai. Hum ek `TIMEOUT` set karenge. Agar data `TIMEOUT` se zyada purana hai, toh contract reject karega. Strict rule: "Purana maal mat ghusaao."

```solidity
uint256 private constant TIMEOUT = 3 hours; // 3 ghante purana data nahi chahiye

if (block.timestamp - updatedAt > TIMEOUT) {
    revert StaleDataError();
}
```

Samjha? Nahi samajh? Padh dubara, dimag lagaa. Ye security ka core hai. Chapter 6 mein hum security pe aur utarenge, but ye basic hygiene hai.

### Market Resolve Karne Ka Khel
Yaad kar **Module 2** ka blueprint. Humne `Market` struct banaya tha. Usme humne `outcome` define kiya tha. Ab hume ek function banan hai jo:
1. Check kare market ka deadline over hai ya nahi.
2. Chainlink se latest data fetch kare.
3. Data staleness check kare.
4. Condition check kare (jaise BTC > $100,000) aur `outcome` set kare.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Smart contract development 20% logic likhna hai, 80% edge cases sochna hai. "Kya hoga agar data nahi aaya?", "Kya hoga agar deadline miss ho gayi?" Ye sochna tere gully ke don jaisa dimag chahiye. Har mod pe goli lag sakti hai, armor pehna ke code likh.

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Chal, ab code pe utarte hain. Theory se pet nahi bharta, asli paise tab aayenge jab code deploy hoga. 

Hum ek `PredictionMarket` contract ka `resolveMarket` function likhenge. Hum assume kar rahe hain ki market pehle se exist karti hai (humne usko `createMarket` se banaya tha). Market ka format simple hai: Outcome 0 = No, Outcome 1 = Yes.

### Step 1: Contract Setup aur Aggregator Initialize
Pehle apne contract ke top pe imports aur variables rakho. Hum custom errors use karenge kyunki **Custom Errors** gas bachati hain (yaad hai Chapter 2 se?).

```solidity
// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity 0.8.20;

import "@chainlink/contracts/src/v0.8/shared/interfaces/AggregatorV3Interface.sol";

error MarketDoesNotExist();
error MarketNotExpired();
error MarketAlreadyResolved();
error StaleDataError();
error InvalidPrice();

contract PredictionMarket {
    // Chapter 2 se aaya hua struct
    struct Market {
        string question;
        uint256 deadline;
        uint256 outcome; // 0 = Unresolved, 1 = Yes, 2 = No
        bool isResolved;
        uint256 targetPrice; // Price jiska check karna hai
    }
    
    mapping(uint256 => Market) public markets;
    uint256 public marketCount;
    
    AggregatorV3Interface public priceFeed;
    uint256 private constant TIMEOUT = 3 hours;

    constructor(address _priceFeedAddress) {
        // Zero Address Check lagana mat bhool, Chapter 3 ka rule!
        if (_priceFeedAddress == address(0)) revert InvalidPrice();
        priceFeed = AggregatorV3Interface(_priceFeedAddress);
    }
    
    // ... (createMarket aur buyShares yahan honge, hum skip kar rahe hain)
}
```

Dekh kya kiya? `AggregatorV3Interface` ko constructor mein address diya. Ab hum is `priceFeed` variable ke through Chainlink ko call kar sakte hain.

### Step 2: Resolve Function Likhna (Asli Jadoo)
Ab wo function likhte hain jo market ka result decide karega.

```solidity
function resolveMarket(uint256 marketId) external {
    Market storage market = markets[marketId];
    
    // 1. Checks and Balances
    if (market.deadline == 0) revert MarketDoesNotExist();
    if (block.timestamp < market.deadline) revert MarketNotExpired();
    if (market.isResolved) revert MarketAlreadyResolved();

    // 2. Chainlink se Data Kheench
    (
        uint80 roundId,
        int256 answer,
        uint256 startedAt,
        uint256 updatedAt,
        uint80 answeredInRound
    ) = priceFeed.latestRoundData();

    // 3. Staleness Check (Sabse zaroori step)
    if (block.timestamp - updatedAt > TIMEOUT) {
        revert StaleDataError();
    }
    
    // 4. Outcome Decide Karo
    // Market question: "Kya BTC price targetPrice se zyada hoga?"
    // answer mein 8 decimals hote hain (Chainlink standard), isliye 1e8 se multiply kiya
    uint256 currentPrice = uint256(answer);
    
    if (currentPrice >= market.targetPrice) {
        market.outcome = 1; // Yes
    } else {
        market.outcome = 2; // No
    }
    
    market.isResolved = true;
}
```

### Code Ka Gyaan: Line by Line Breakdown
Tu soch raha hai "Bhai, itna sa code tha?" Haan saale, smart contracts mein kam code, solid logic. Chal samjhata hoon:

1. **`Market storage market = markets[marketId];`**: Hum `storage` use kar rahe hain kyunki hume market ka data update karna hai (`outcome` aur `isResolved`). Yaad rakh **storage vs memory** ka rule. `memory` sirf copy banata hai, `storage` original data change karta hai.

2. **Checks**: Pehle check kiya market exist karti hai kya. Phir check kiya deadline over hai kya. Agar tu deadline se pehle resolve karega, toh logon ke paise doob jayenge. Phir check kiya pehle se resolved toh nahi hai. Ek market ko do baar resolve karna gunda gardi hai.

3. **`priceFeed.latestRoundData()`**: Yahan Chainlink ko call maara. Data nikal liya. `roundId` aur `startedAt` hum use nahi kar rahe, but hume unhe declare karna padta hai syntax ke liye.

4. **Staleness Check**: `block.timestamp - updatedAt > TIMEOUT`. `block.timestamp` current block ka time hai. `updatedAt` Chainlink ne jab data daala tha. Agar difference 3 ghante se zyada hai, toh goli maaro, data purana hai. `revert StaleDataError()` chal jayega.

5. **`uint256 currentPrice = uint256(answer);`**: Chainlink `int256` mein price deta hai. Humne usko `uint256` mein cast kiya kyunki price negative nahi hota. **Warning:** Yahan dhyan de. Agar Chainlink kabhi negative value de (which is rare for price feeds), toh ye cast fail ho sakta hai. Lekin price feeds ke liye ye safe hai.

6. **Outcome Set**: `currentPrice >= market.targetPrice`. Agar price target se zyada hai, toh `outcome = 1` (Yes), warna `outcome = 2` (No). `isResolved = true` set karke market band kar diya.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Chainlink price feeds 8 decimals mein aate hain. Agar tu `targetPrice` USD mein set kar raha hai, toh usko bhi 8 decimals mein store kar. Jaise $100,000 = 100000 * 1e8. Warnu comparison galat ho jayega aur tu gaali khaayega.

### Step 3: Edge Case - Agar Chainlink Hi Mar Gayi Toh?
Bhai, gully ka rule: "Umeed pe duniya chalti hai, par backup plan rakhna padta hai." 

Maan le Chainlink ka wo specific feed 24 ghante ke liye update nahi hua. Staleness check fail ho jayega. Market resolve nahi hoga. Logon ke paise atak jayenge. Kya karega?

Iske liye ek emergency exit banana chahiye. Jaise agar market 7 din se unresolved hai, toh admin (ya koi bhi) usko "Draw" ya "Cancel" kar sake aur logon ke paise wapas kar sake. 

Ye hum Module 5 (Claim) aur Module 6 (Security) mein detail mein dekhenge. Abhi ke liye bas samajh le: **Smart contract mein har external dependency ke liye ek backup plan hona chahiye.**

### Step 4: Testing Is Shit (Foundry/Hardhat)
Agar tu bina test kiye ye code mainnet pe daala, toh tu worthy hai ek thappad ke. Chapter 3 mein humne **Foundry** aur **testCreateMarket** jaise functions dekhe the. 

Chainlink testing ke liye hume ek mock (fake) oracle banana padta hai. Mainnet pe real Chainlink address use hota hai, testnet pe testnet address. Par local testing ke liye hum ek fake contract banate hain jo `latestRoundData()` return kare.

```solidity
contract MockChainlinkOracle {
    int256 public price;
    uint256 public updatedAt;
    
    constructor(int256 _price) {
        price = _price;
        updatedAt = block.timestamp;
    }
    
    function latestRoundData() external view returns (
        uint80, int256, uint256, uint256, uint80

```

Apne test file mein is mock ko deploy kar, uska address `PredictionMarket` constructor mein daal, aur phir `resolveMarket` call kar. 
Test cases likh:
1. `testResolveMarketSuccess`: Jab price target se upar hai.
2. `testResolveMarketFailure`: Jab price target se neeche hai.
3. `testStaleDataRevert`: `updatedAt` ko purana karke revert check kar.
4. `testResolveBeforeDeadline`: `block.timestamp` pehle se set karke revert check kar.

Agar ye 4 test pass ho gaye, toh samajh tera contract 50% solid hai.

# Module 5: Khel Khatam Paisa Bat: Market Resolve Aur Claim

Abe sun, tera startup ka idea fail ho gaya, investor ne paisa nahi diya, aur tu raat 3 baje ro raha tha ki "Bhai kya hi zindagi hai." Saale, ek cheez miss kar gaya tu — execution. Idea koi bhi de sakta hai, execution karna sabse mushkil hai. Prediction market bhi same hai. Tu market bana, log paise lagayein, sab mast chal raha hai — lekin jab result aana hota hai, tab asli game hota hai. Wahan tu fail hua toh poora system g**nd mara. Chapter 4 mein tu Chainlink se data kheench na seekha, ab us data se winner decide karna hai aur paise baatna hai. Yeh hai asli settlement ka scene.

Aaj ka chapter hai — "Khel Khatam Paisa Bat: Market Resolve Aur Claim". Bhai, agar yeh chapter skip kiya toh tera contract ek dhaal (shield) hai jismein hole itna bada hoga ki hacker tera paisa kha jayega aur tu chup-chap apni mummy ko bhi nahi bata paayega. Ready? Chal biryani order kar, chai mangwa, aur dhyaan se padh.

---

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

Dekh bhai, ye settlement ka process do hisson mein bata hua hai. Ek hai *Resolve* (winner pata karna), aur doosra hai *Claim* (paise baantna). Jaise IPL ke final ke baad pehle trophy decide hoti hai (Resolve), fir team walon ko prize money milti hai (Claim). Dono alag kaam hai, dono ko mix mat kar warna gadbad ho jayegi.

### 1. ResolveMarket: Winner Ka Faisla

Abey, soch. Market ban gaya (Chapter 2), logon ne shares khareed liye (Chapter 3), Chainlink oracle ne bahar ka data andar kheench liya (Chapter 4). Ab market ko "resolve" karna hai. Iska matlab? Market ki final outcome decide karna. Jaise, "Kya 2024 mein India World Cup jitega?" iska answer "Yes" ya "No" hona chahiye. Ye decision sirf owner lega — kyunki market maker bhai tu hi hai, aur tu hi Chainlink data dekh ke decide karega ki aram se.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** `ResolveMarket` function mein `onlyOwner` modifier lagana **mat bhulna**. Agar tu ye bhool gaya, toh koi bhi random aadmi tere market ko resolve kar dega aur winners ko paise baant dega. BC, tera market nahi, uska market ban jayega.

Dekh iska code. Humne Chapter 2 mein `Struct aur Mappings` se market ka blueprint banaya tha. Wahi use hoga.

```solidity
// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.19;

contract PredictionMarket {
    // Chapter 2 se yaad kar, wahi struct
    struct Market {
        string question;
        uint256 deadline;
        bool isResolved;
        uint256 winningOutcome; // 0 = No, 1 = Yes
        uint256 totalPool;      // Total paisa pool
        uint256 yesPool;        // Yes pe lagaya hua paisa
        uint256 noPool;         // No pe lagaya hua paisa
    }

    mapping(uint256 => Market) public markets;
    uint256 public marketCount;

    // Chapter 3 se: User ne kya kitna buy kiya
    mapping(uint256 => mapping(address => uint256)) public userShares;
    
    // Naya mapping: User ne claim kiya ya nahi?
    mapping(uint256 => mapping(address => bool)) public hasClaimed;

    address public owner;

    constructor() {
        owner = msg.sender;
    }

    modifier onlyOwner() {
        require(msg.sender == owner, "Saale, tu owner nahi hai!");
        _;
    }

    // ... (Create aur Buy functions Chapter 2 & 3 mein already likhe)
    
    function resolveMarket(uint256 marketId, uint256 _winningOutcome) external onlyOwner {
        Market storage market = markets[marketId];
        
        // Market pehle resolve na ho
        require(!market.isResolved, "Bhai, ek baar resolve ho gaya, ab mat chhed");
        
        // Deadline pass ho chuki honi chahiye
        require(block.timestamp > market.deadline, "BC, match khatam bhi hua hai ki nahi?");
        
        // Outcome valid hona chahiye (0 ya 1)
        require(_winningOutcome <= 1, "Abe 0 ya 1, aur kya outcome hai?");

        market.winningOutcome = _winningOutcome;
        market.isResolved = true;
    }
}
```

Samjha? Nahi? Padh dubara BC. `resolveMarket` function mein humne `onlyOwner` lagaya. Owner aayega, market ki ID dega, aur winning outcome (0 ya 1) batayega. `require(!market.isResolved)` check lagaya ki ek hi market do baar resolve na ho jaye. Agar tu ye check nahi dega, toh koi hacker aayega, market ko doosri baar resolve karega, aur paise losers ko baant dega. `block.timestamp > market.deadline` check lagaya ki market ka deadline pass ho chuka ho. Agar tu ye check nahi dega, toh market start hote hi resolve ho jayega, aur logon ka paisa doob jayega. Ye function sirf state update karta hai, paise baatne ka kaam yahan nahi karenge. Paise hum `claimWinnings` mein baatenge.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Lode, agar tu `resolveMarket` mein hi paise transfer karne lagta hai, toh tu `Reentrancy` attack ka shikar ho jayega. Pehle state update karo, fir paise nikalo. Yahan hum sirf state update kar rahe hain, isliye safe hai. Paise baatne ka kaam `claimWinnings` mein hoga, wahan safety ke pehle daale lagenge.

### 2. ClaimWinnings: Paise Lo Aur Nikal Lo

Bhai, ab asli khel. Market resolve ho gaya. Winning side pata hai. Ab winners ko paise baatne hain. 

Dekh, paise baatne ka funda simple hai — **Proportional Distribution**. Jaise autorickshaw mein 3 log baithte hain, aur meter ka paisa ₹150 hai. Agar sabne barabar paisa lagaya hai, toh ₹50-50 denge. Lekin agar ek bande ne ₹100 lagaye aur doosre ne ₹50, toh pehle wale ko zyada milega. Yahan bhi same hai. Jo banda `Yes` pool mein zyada paisa lagayega, wo zyada share lega winning pool se.

**Mathematics samajh:**
Suppose `Yes` pool = 100 ETH (total 100 shares, 1 ETH per share)
`No` pool = 50 ETH (total 50 shares, 1 ETH per share)
Total Pool = 150 ETH

Agar `Yes` win karta hai, toh `Yes` wale 150 ETH baantenge. Ek bande ke paas 10 shares hain (10 ETH lagaye the). Toh uska share = (10 / 100) * 150 = 15 ETH. Usne 10 ETH lagaye the, 5 ETH profit. Bhai, 50% return! Sharma ji ke bete ke FD se zyada!

Lekin ek problem hai. Agar tu loop laga ke saare winners ko paise baantne ki koshish karega, toh Gas fee itni zyada aayegi ki tera pura profit gas fee mein ud jayega. Bhai, Ethereum pe loop chalana is like trying to fill an ocean with a spoon. Agar 10,000 logon ne bet lagayi hai, aur tu ek function mein loop chala raha hai unko paise baatne ke liye, toh Gas Limit exceed ho jayegi aur transaction revert ho jayegi. Poora contract jam (block) jayega.

Isliye, hum **Pull over Push** pattern use karenge. Yani hum paise khud nahi baantenge. User ko apna paisa claim karne ke liye aana padega. Jaise bank mein FD todne ke liye khud jaana padta hai, na ki bank khud ghar pe paisa dene aata hai.

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** **Pull over Push** pattern is the street-smart way. Contract paise khud push nahi karega. User khud aayega `claimWinnings()` call karke. Isse gas cost distribute ho jati hai (har user apna gas pay karega), aur reentrancy attacks se bachne mein madad milti hai.

Chal, code dekh:

```solidity
    function claimWinnings(uint256 marketId) external {
        Market storage market = markets[marketId];
        
        // Market resolved hona chahiye
        require(market.isResolved, "Bhai, market abhi khatam nahi hua, kya claim kar raha hai?");
        
        // User ne pehle claim nahi kiya hona chahiye
        require(!hasClaimed[marketId][msg.sender], "Saale, ek baar paisa le liya, ab kya ro raha hai?");
        
        // User ke paas shares hone chahiye
        uint256 userSharesCount = userShares[marketId][msg.sender];
        require(userSharesCount > 0, "Tere paas toh koi share hi nahi hai, kya lena?");
        
        // Winning side check karo
        uint256 winningOutcome = market.winningOutcome;
        bool isUserWinner = (winningOutcome == 1 && userSharesCount > 0); // Simplified logic
        
        // Actually, userShares mapping mein Yes/No alag se track karna padega.
        // Humne Chapter 3 mein Buy Shares kiya tha. Wahan humne userShares[marketId][msg.sender] track kiya tha.
        // Let's assume userShares mapping mein user ne kya outcome pe kitna paisa lagaya hai, wo stored hai.
        // For simplicity, assume karte hain ki humare paas do mappings hain: yesShares aur noShares
        // (Code ko clean rakhne ke liye, yahan conceptual logic dikha raha hu)

        uint256 payout = 0;
        
        if (winningOutcome == 1) {
            // Yes won. User ke Yes shares.
            // payout = (User's Yes Shares / Total Yes Shares) * Total Pool
            // Yahan hume user's yes shares aur total yes shares chahiye.
            // Chapter 3 ki mapping yaad kar, userShares[marketId][msg.sender] = shares.
            // Hum assume karte hain ki userShares sirf winning side ke shares track karta hai.
            // Agar user ne Yes pe lagaya hai aur Yes won, toh:
            
            // payout = (userSharesCount * market.totalPool) / market.yesPool;
            payout = (userSharesCount * market.totalPool) / market.yesPool;
        } else {
            // No won
            payout = (userSharesCount * market.totalPool) / market.noPool;
        }
        
        // Mark as claimed BEFORE transfer (Reentrancy guard!)
        hasClaimed[marketId][msg.sender] = true;
        
        // Paise do
        (bool success, ) = msg.sender.call{value: payout}("");
        require(success, "Paise transfer nahi hue, BC network kharab hai kya?");
    }
```

Samjha? Nahi? Padh dubara BC.

Dekh, yahan `claimWinnings` function mein user apna paisa claim karne aayega. `require(market.isResolved)` check karega ki market resolve ho chuka hai. `require(!hasClaimed[marketId][msg.sender])` check karega ki user ne dobara claim na kare. `userSharesCount` check karega ki user ke paas shares hain ya nahi. Agar user ne winning side pe bet lagayi hai, toh usko proportional payout milega.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Bhai, `hasClaimed` mapping ko `true` set karna **transfer se pehle** karna hai. Agar tu paise transfer karne ke baad `hasClaimed` ko `true` karta hai, toh hacker `Reentrancy` attack karke baar-baar paisa nikal lega. Ye Chapter 6 ki warning hai, lekin yahi pe kaam aati hai. **Checks-Effects-Interactions** pattern. Pehle checks lagao, fir state (effects) update karo, fir paise (interactions) transfer karo. Yaad rakh, nahi toh ghar ki lakshmi bahar chali jayegi.

### 3. Loser Ka Paisa: Jugaad Math

Bhai, asli math yahan hai. Winner ko loser ka paisa baatna hai. Jaise arranged marriage mein ladke aur ladki ki family baith ke "ladke wale itna denge, ladki wale itna lenge" decide karti hain. Yahan bhi proportional distribution hai.

**Formula:**
`User Payout = (User's Shares in Winning Pool / Total Shares in Winning Pool) * Total Pool (Yes + No)`

Suppose tu "Yes" pe 10 ETH lagata hai. Total Yes pool 100 ETH hai. No pool 50 ETH hai. Total pool 150 ETH hai. Agar Yes win karta hai, toh tera payout = (10 / 100) * 150 = 15 ETH. Terre 10 ETH me 15 ETH mil gaye, 5 ETH profit.

Agar tu "Yes" pe 10 ETH lagata hai, total Yes pool 100 ETH hai, No pool 50 ETH hai. Total pool 150 ETH. Agar No win karta hai, toh Yes walo ka paisa No walo ko mil jayega. Terre 10 ETH doob gaye, BC. Ye life hai bhai, kabhi kabhi haar bhi milti hai.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Lode, ye formula `User Payout = (User's Shares / Total Pool) * Total Pool` use mat kar. Agar tu ye karega, toh winner ko sirf apna paisa milega, loser ka paisa nahi. Winner ka faayda hi nahi raha phir! Winner ko loser ka paisa baatna hai, isliye `Total Pool` (Yes + No) se multiply karo, aur `Winning Pool` se divide karo. Samjha? Nahi? Padh dubara BC.

###  ResolveMarket aur Chainlink: Data Ka Asli Khel

Bhai, Chapter 4 mein tu Chainlink oracle ka jadu seekha tha. Chainlink se data kheench na, usko resolve market ke liye use karna hai. Chainlink oracle `fulfillRequest` function mein data laata hai. Wahan se `resolveMarket` ko call karna hai? Nahi bhai, nahi. Chainlink `fulfillRequest` se direct market resolve karna. Oracle data laayega, aur `resolveMarket` function ko call karna hai? Nahi bhai, nahi. Chainlink `fulfillRequest` se direct market resolve karna.

Dekh, Chainlink oracle data laata hai. Us data se tu winner decide karega. Agar tu manual `resolveMarket` call karta hai, toh tu Chainlink data dekh ke manually winner decide karega. Lekin asli decentralized prediction market mein, oracle khud market resolve karta hai. Iske liye `fulfillRequest` function ke andar hi `resolveMarket` logic likhna padega.

Suppose Chainlink API returns `1` for Yes and `0` for Market. Tu `fulfillRequest` mein data parse karega, aur market ko resolve karega. Yahan `onlyOwner` nahi, `onlyChainlinkOracle` modifier lagega. Agar tu `onlyOwner` laga dega, toh Chainlink call karte hi transaction revert ho jayegi, kyunki Chainlink oracle ka address tere owner address se alag hota. Samjha? Nahi? Padh dubara BC.

```solidity
// Chainlink fulfillRequest function (Conceptual - Chapter 4 ka extension)
function fulfillRequest(
    bytes32 requestId,
    uint256 data
) public recordChainlinkFulfillment(requestId) {
    // data 1 hai toh Yes, 0 hai toh No
    uint256 outcome = data;
    
    // Market resolve karo
    // Yahan directly market state update karo, resolveMarket function ko call mat karo
    // kyunki resolveMarket onlyOwner laga hai, aur Chainlink oracle owner nahi hai.
    Market storage market = markets[marketId]; // marketId ko request ke saath pass karna padega
    require(!market.isResolved, "Already resolved");
    require(block.timestamp > market.deadline, "Deadline not passed");
    
    market.winningOutcome = outcome;
    market.isResolved = true;
    
    // Emit event
}
```

Bhai, yahan ek problem hai. `fulfillRequest` ko `marketId` chahiye. Lekin Chainlink request bhejne ke baad, response aane tak `marketId` track karna mushkil hota hay. Iske liye `requestId` ko `marketId` se map karna padta hai. Jaise tu Swiggy pe order karta hai, aur order ID se track karta hai ki kaunsa order aa gaya. Yahan `requestId` = order ID, `marketId` = tera biryani. `mapping(bytes32 => uint256) public requestToMarket;` use karna padega. Jab request bhejoge, toh `requestToMarket[requestId] = marketId;` save kar lena. Jab response aayega, toh `marketId = requestToMarket[requestId];` se nikal lena.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Chainlink `fulfillRequest` function ko `onlyOwner` mat bana. Bhai, Chainlink oracle tere contract ka owner nahi hota. Agar tu `onlyOwner` laga dega, toh Chainlink call karte hi transaction revert ho jayega, kyunki Chainlink oracle ka address tere owner address se alag hai. Isliye `recordChainlinkFulfillment` modifier laga hota hai, jo Chainlink node se aayi request ko verify karta hai. Chapter 4 mein humne ye dala tha, yaad rakh.

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Bhai, theory toh tu padh gaya. Ab dekh practical mein kya hota hai. Tu contract deploy karta hai. Log paise lagate hain. Market resolve hota hai. User claim karta hai. Lekin asli duniya mein problems aati hain. Chal, dekhte hain.

### Scenario 1: Bhai Ne Paise Lagaye, Lekin Market Resolve Nahi Hua

Ravi ne 0.5 ETH lagaye "Yes" pe. Market ka deadline pass ho gaya, lekin owner (tu) Chainlink request trigger nahi kiya, ya Chainlink oracle down hai. Ravi ro raha hai. "Bhai, mera paisa doob gaya!" 

**Solution:** Bhai, ye design flaw hai. Agar owner manual resolve karta hai, toh owner lazy ho sakta hai. Isliye `resolveMarket` function mein ek `autoResolve` mechanism bana sakte hain. Jaise hi deadline pass ho, koi bhi user Chainlink request trigger kar sake. Iske liye `triggerResolve` function bana, jo `onlyOwner` nahi, `anyone` call kar sake. Lekin gas fee caller ko deni padegi. Isliye caller ko small reward de sakte hain. Jaise Swiggy pe delivery boy ko tip dete hain.

### Scenario 2: Hacker Bhaiya Reentrancy Attack

Market resolve ho gaya. Hacker ne "Yes" pe 10 ETH lagaye. Hacker ke paas 10 shares hain. Hacker `claimWinnings` call karta hai. Contract check karta hai ki hacker ke paas 10 shares hain, payout 15 ETH hai. Contract `hasClaimed` ko `true` set karta hai. Lekin hacker ka address ek smart contract hai! Hacker ke contract ka `receive()` function mein `claimWinnings` dubara call karne ka code likha hai. 

Jab contract hacker ko 15 ETH transfer karta hai, toh hacker ka `receive()` function trigger hota hai. `receive()` function `claimWinings` dubara call karta hai. Lekin `hasClaimed` pehle hi `true` ho chuka hai! Toh `require(!hasClaimed)` fail ho jayega. Hacker ka attack rok diya `Checks-Effects-Interactions` pattern ne. Bhai, ye pattern is like wearing a seatbelt. Accident nahi hoga hopefully, lekin agar hua toh tu bach gaya. Chapter 6 mein iska aur deep dive lenge, lekin yahan base set kar diya.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Saale, `msg.sender.call{value: payout}("")` use karte time `hasClaimed` ko `true` **transfer se pehle** set karna. Agar tu transfer ke baad set karta hai, toh hacker ke `receive()` function se `claimWinnings` dubara call hoga, aur `hasClaimed` abhi false hai, toh paisa dubara nikal jayega. BC, poora contract khali ho jayega. Checks-Effects-Interactions. Yaad rakh.

### Scenario  Abe, Gas Limit Exceeded!

10,000 logon ne bet lagayi. Market resolve ho gaya. Ab log claim kar rahe hain. Lekin agar tu loop laga ke saare logon ko paise baatne ki koshish karega (Push pattern), toh Gas Limit exceed ho jayegi. Transaction revert. Contract jam. Bhai, ye wahi scene hai jab Mumbai local train mein 5 baje peak hour mein chadhne ki koshish karta hai. Kitne logon ko push karoge? Ek-ek karke aana padta hai. Isliye Pull pattern use karta. User khud aayega, apna gas pay karega, aur apna paisa claim karega.

### Scenario 4: Division by Zero

Bhai, agar `yesPool` ya `noPool` 0 hai, toh payout calculate karte time division by zero error aayega. Agar kisi ne "No" pe bet nahi lagayi, aur `noPool` 0 hai, toh `payout = (userSharesCount * market.totalPool) / market.noPool;` error dega. Isliye `resolveMarket` mein check lagana ki dono pools me thoda toh paisa hona chahiye. Ya phir `claimWinnings` mein check lagana ki jis side ne win kiya, us pool mein paisa ho.

```solidity
// resolveMarket mein check lagao
require(market.yesPool > 0 && market.noPool > 0, "Bhai, kisi ne bet nahi lagayi kya?");
```

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Division by zero Solidity 0.8.x mein revert karta hai, lekin tu chup chap betha rahega ki error kahan hai. Isliye pool sizes check karne ke checks lagao. Debugging is like finding a needle in a haystack. Agar tu `require` message nahi dega, toh user ko pata hi nahi chalega ki error kya hai. Clear error messages do, BC.

### Scenario 5: Dust Amounts

Bhai, Solidity mein integer division decimal ko support nahi karta. Jaise 10 / 3 = 3.33, lekin Solidity mein 10 / 3 = 3. Yahan bhi payout calculate karte time kuch paisa dust (remainder) ke roop me contract mein bach jayega. Jaise 100 ETH ko 3 logon mein baatna ho, toh 33.33 ETH per head, lekin Solidity mein 33 ETH per head, 1 ETH bach jayega. Ye 1 ETH contract mein trapped rahega. Isko "dust" bolte hain. Bhai, ye dust amount ko owner baad mein withdraw kar sakta hai, ya phir ignore kar sakte hain. Agar tu perfect banna chahta hai, toh math precision library use kar sakte hain, lekin gas zyada lagega. Jugaad: Dust ko ignore kar, bhai. Jaise chai ki tapri pe ₹5 chai pe ₹2 udhaar de, toh wo udhaar kabhi wapas nahi aata, ignore kar.

### Scenario 6: Chainlink Oracle Se Galat Data

Bhai, Chainlink oracle se data aata hai. Oracle ne `1` return kiya (Yes won). Lekin asli duniya mein "No" won. Oracle galat data de raha hai. Ab kya? Bhai, ye decentralized oracles ki limitation hai. Agar oracle galat data deta hai, toh market galat resolve hoga. Isliye multiple oracles use karna chahiye, ya phir dispute mechanism bana sakte hain. Jaise court mein appeal hoti hai, waisa hi. Lekin ye sab advanced level hai. Abhi ke liye, assume kar ki Chainlink sahi data deta hai. Chapter 7 mein testing pe iska scene palatenge.

### Scenario 7: User Akela, Pool Khali

Bhai, ek aur edge case. Suppose ek hi user ne "Yes" pe 10 ETH lagaye. Kisi ne "No" pe bet nahi lagayi. `noPool` = 0. Total pool = 10 ETH. Market resolve hua, "Yes" won. User claim karta hai. Payout = (10 / 10) * 10 = 10 ETH. User ko apna hi paisa mil gaya, koi profit nahi. Bhai, ye toh bekar hai! Isliye market creation ke time check lagana ki dono sides pe kam se kam thoda paisa ho. Ya phir market creation ke time minimum bet amount set kar. Jaise minimum ₹100 ki bet lagani padti hai. Warna log aayenge, ek side pe 1 wei lagayenge, aur market resolve ho jayega. Bekar.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Bhai, prediction market ka asli challenge ye nahi ki code kaise likha hai. Asli challenge ye hai ki edge cases handle karo. Hacker tere contract ke har corner case ko test karega. Tu chahiye shaatir, hacker se bhi zyada shaatir. Har edge case ke liye `require` check lagao. Har state change ke liye event emit karo. Har transfer ke liye `Checks-Effects-Interactions` follow karo. Ye sab muscle memory ban jaye, tab tu asli smart contract developer hai. Nahi toh tu sirf "Sharma ji ka beta" hai jo syntax likh sakta hai, lekin system design nahi kar sakta.
